history.content:
<thought>
I am facing right (`>`). The cell directly ahead is lava, so I should not move forward. I need to head downward to reach the open path toward the goal, so I will turn clockwise first.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at the left side of the map facing down. The goal `O` is near the lower-right area, and the row below me is open to the right after turning. Moving down would go into open space, so the safest progress is to face right and head toward the goal along the clear path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing left near the upper-left area, but the cell directly to my left is a wall, so moving forward is impossible. I need to head right toward the open corridor and eventually to the goal near the lower-right, so I will turn right to face upward/downward progression? Actually from left-facing, a right turn makes me face up, which is also blocked by the wall, so the better first step is to turn around toward the open interior by turning left? From left-facing, turning left makes me face down, and the cell below is open.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am at the left side of the map facing down, with open floor to my right and the goal located near the lower-right area. Moving forward would go into a wall, so I should first turn to face right and then navigate along the open path toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing left near the top-left area. The cell directly ahead is a wall, so I should not move forward. The goal is on the lower-right side, so I need to reorient to the right and then proceed through the open corridor. The first step is to turn clockwise.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at the top-left area facing up, and the goal `O` is near the bottom-right. The cell directly ahead is a wall, so I cannot move forward. I need to start by turning right to face the open path along the top row.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at the left side of the top open corridor, facing right. The goal `O` is far to the lower-right, and the path ahead is blocked by lava at the next cell, so I should not move forward yet. I need to take a safer route by going downward first, so I will turn right to face down.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am near the left side of the map and facing down. The cell directly ahead is empty, so I can make progress downward toward the open corridor and eventually the goal at the lower-right. Moving forward is safe and advances me.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am near the lower-left area, facing down (`v`). The goal `O` is on the far right in the same row as me, so I should start heading right. To face right from down, I need to turn counter-clockwise once.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am on the left side of the map facing right (`>`). The goal `O` is near the bottom-right, and the path ahead in my row is blocked by lava at the next cell, so I need to start moving downward toward the open corridor below.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am near the left side, facing up. The goal `O` is in the lower-right area. The safest path appears to be moving down along the left/open corridor, then across the open row toward the goal. First I need to face down, so I will turn left from up to left? Actually from `^`, turning right would face `>`, while turning left would face `<`. To move downward, I need two turns, but the best next step is to turn right once toward the open space and start aligning toward the corridor.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing right (`>`), and the goal `O` is visible on the same bottom row, far to the right. The path along this row is open, so the next step is to move forward toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
